rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH PALELLA VS. SAN FRANCISCO PRODUCE ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO PRODUCE ASSOCIATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied. Defendants met their burden of production by making a prima facie showing that there are no triable issues of material fact. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850.) This shifted the burden to Plaintiff to show a triable issue of material fact. (Id.) Plaintiff has met this burden by establishing that there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the harm of this sort was foreseeable. Although foreseeability is not the only factor in determining duty, it "plays a very significant role" in the analysis. (Lawson v. Safeway (2010) 191 Cal.App.4th 400, 409.) While duty is a question of law, the issue of foreseeability is a question of fact. (Bigbee v. Pacific Tel. & Tel. Co. (1983) 34 Cal.3d 49, 56.) It may be decided as a question of law only if, "under the undisputed facts there is no room for a reasonable difference of opinion." (Id., citing Schrimscher v. Bryson (1976) 58 Cal.App.3d 660, 664.) Here, Plaintiff has established there is a reasonable difference of opinion. Plaintiff has produced evidence showing that cars drove over the "island," cars had run off of the road and onto the "island" at least twice previously, and Defendants considered measures to prevent cars from entering the "island." (DMF no. 23; AMF nos. 37 and 45.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: RICHARD S RESMA et al VS. UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYDS OF LONDON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Reconsideration Of Summary Judgment And For Leave To Amend Complaint To Conform To Proof
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 8, PLAINTIFFs RICHARD RESMA and CYNTHIA RESMA's motion For Reconsideration Of Summary Judgment And For Leave To Amend Complaint To Conform To Proof Plaintiffs' motions to reconsider and to amend are denied. The Court, having already entered judgment, can no longer entertain these motions. The Court declines Plaintiffs' invitation in their reply brief to treat these motions as requests to vacate the judgment under CCP section 473(b). Such a request is both procedurally improper and substantively unsupported. Even if the Court could consider the motion to reconsider, it would deny it. Plaintiffs had the allegedly new information before filing their opposition and well before hearing on the motion. Were it essential to their opposition, they needed to bring it to the Court's attention then. In any event, the allegedly new information would not change the outcome. It does not support a viable basis for tolling the statute of limitations that began running once the checks were issued. If a timely notice to appear to contest this tentative ruling is given, the hearing will take place in Department 504 before Judge Peter J. Busch at 9:00 a.m. on Monday, November 25, 2013. The court will not provide a court reporter for this hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg

rulings.Case Title: GILBERTO CORDOVA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT JMB CONSTRUCTION, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Denied. (CCP § 437c.) Defendant has not met its initial burden of proof. Defendant moves for summary judgment, or alternatively summary adjudication, on the grounds that: (1) Plaintiff cannot prove an essential element of his two causes of action that Plaintiff had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition; and (2) Defendant's evidence demonstrates that it did not have actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition, negating an essential element of Plaintiff's claim. (Notice of Motion, p. 2:3-12.) Both causes of action are predicated on negligence. (Complaint, GN-1, Prem.L-2.) Actual or constructive knowledge is not an element of negligence or general premises liability. (CACI nos. 400, 1000.) Defendant has not met is initial burden to "conclusively negate a necessary element of the plaintiff's case or establish a complete defense and thereby demonstrate that under no hypothesis is there a material factual issue which requires the process of a trial." (Brooks v. Eugene Burger Management Corp. (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 1611, 1618.) rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton For Protective Orderstaying Or, Alternatively, Limiting Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 5 - DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED's Moiton For Protective Orderstaying Or, Alternatively, Limiting Discovery. Part 1. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.

rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH VS. FOOD MARKET MANAGEMENT, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Mccormick & Company'S Notice Of Moiton For Protective Orderstaying Or, Alternatively, Limiting Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 5 - DEFENDANT MCCORMICK & COMPANY, INCORPORATED's Moiton For Protective Orderstaying Or, Alternatively, Limiting Discovery. Part 2. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. In this action Plaintiff alleges that the various Defendants manufacture distribute, and sell lead-containing "ginger and plum baking ingredients"without providing warnings that Plaintiff maintains are required by California's Proposition 65, Health & Safety Code Section 25249.5 et seq. Defendant McCormick & Company seeks a stay of all discovery pending the outcome of an appeal from a decision of the Alameda County Superior Court that Proposition 65 did not require Beech-Nut Corporation and the other defendants in that litigation to give consumers warnings that the defendants' fruit, vegetable and grape drink products contain lead. McCormick & Company argues that a stay is appropriate in this litigation concerning candied baking ingredients because the court has the power to exercise its inherent authority to insure the orderly administration of justice, and the power under CCP Sections 2019.020(b) to establish the sequence and timing of discovery. A review of the Alameda County Superior Court's July 31, 2013 statement of decision (Ex. A to Somers Decl. In Support of Opposition to Motion for Protective Order) shows that the basis for the Superior Court's ruling was that the evidence demonstrated that the Beech-Nut defendants were entitled to the Health and Safety Code Section 25249.10(c) safe-harbor defense because the juices at issue did not expose the average consumer to a threshold level of lead, The Alameda Court's decision was grounded on a highly fact-specific inquiry concerning the juices at issue in that litigation and the appeal of the court's ruling on that question does not provide a basis for staying discovery in litigation concerning lead in different food products. Although the question of federal preemption of Proposition 65 may be an issue in the Alameda appeal, that does not provide a basis for staying other instances of Proposition 65 litigation in Superior Courts around the state. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Deft Bank Of The West' Subpoenas And Request For A Monetary Sanction In The Amount Of $2415 Against Deft And Its Counsel Littler Mendelson Pc
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 13, PLAINTIFF NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL's Motion To Quash Defendant Bank Of The West' Subpoenas And Request For A Monetary Sanction In The Amount Of $2415 Against Defendant And Its Counsel Littler Mendelson Pc Granted, in part as the subpoenas for medical and dental records. The description of the DOCUMENTS sought will be modified to read as follows: (1) any preexisting ulcerous condition of NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL, or (2) any clinical depression of NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL, or (3) any Documents that describe any medical condition, course of treatment, or medication that contributed to or impacted a pre-existing ulcerous condition or clinical depression of NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL.¿ Granted in part and denied in part as to the subpoena for the employment records of Bank of America. The only records for which the defendant's interests overcome the privacy objections concern the last year of employment and any "for cause" grounds for termination of employment. Accordingly, the motion is granted as to all employment records that pre-date the final year of employment, and the motion is denied as to the limited request for records during the last year of employment or that concern the grounds for termination of employment in the last year. Assuming that the date of termination was May 29, 2002, the description of the records sought is modified to include only those records for the last year of employment and would read as follows: "Any and all records from May 29, 2001 and all records related to NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL's termination of employment in 2002." Granted as to the motion to quash the subpoena on the Custodian of Records for Bank of the West for the short sale documents. Defendants failed to demonstrate a nexus between any financial disclosures made in connection with a short sale application and the damages sought. Defendants already have a copy of the short sale application from the plaintiff. As to the claim for set-off for forgiven debt, defendants offers no legal basis for such a defense. Finally, even assuming such an offset could be lawfully applied, the information for the amount of the offset is in the possession of the bank and does not depend upon production of the consumer's records to be ascertained. The request for sanctions is denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: GUILLERMO SIERRA VS. CARPET CONTRACTORS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANTS CARPET CONTRACTORS, INC., NICHOLAS BOTAITIS AND DEMETRI BOTAITIS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Granted. (CCP § 436.) The addition of a new party plaintiff exceeds the scope of the leave to amend granted after the sustaining of a demurrer as to Mr. Sierra's claims. (Harris v. Wachovia Mortg., FSB (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 1018, 1023; People ex rel. Dept. Pub. Wks. v. Clausen (1967) 248 Cal.App.2d 770, 785.) Plaintiffs' reliance on La Sala v. American Sav. & Loan Assn. (1971) 5 Cal.3d 864 and Pirjada v. Superior Court (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 1074 are distinguishable from the instant action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: CACH, LLC VS. BRIAN SEPP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Order That Matters In Req For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 8 - PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC's Motion For Order That Matters In Req For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. The address on the proof of service for Plaintiff's motion and for Plaintiff's request for admissions set number one does not match the address listed by defendant Brian Sepp on the answer he filed with the court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: TONY NG PLUMBING COMPANY, A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP VS. CAL-PACIFIC CONSTRUCTION, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And For Stay Of Proceedings Pending Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 20, DEFENDANT CAL-PACIFIC CONSTRUCTION, INCORPORATED'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And For Stay Of Proceedings Pending Arbitration Defendant's petition to compel arbitration is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The record shows the parties formed a valid and enforceable agreement to arbitrate, and the arbitration clause encompasses the underlying dispute. Plaintiff has not carried its burden of showing that Defendant waived its' right to arbitrate the dispute in accordance with the Subcontract Agreement. (See Saint Agnes Med. Ctr. v. PacifiCare of Calif. (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195.) The present action is stayed until arbitration is completed. (CCP § 1281.4.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EHG

rulings.Case Title: YELP, INC A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. MCMILLIAN LAW GROUP INC.*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN DIEGO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Action To San Diego County; And For Pymt By Pltf'S Councel Of Def'S Expenses And Attny'S Fees Incurred
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, November 25, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANTs MCMILLIAN LAW GROUP INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION and JULIAN MCMILLIAN's motion To Transfer Action To San Diego County; And For Payment By Plaintiff's Counsel Of Defendant'S Expenses And Attorneys Fees Incurred Granted. Venue is transferred to San Diego County pursuant to CCP § 395(a) and Plaintiff's non-opposition. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant reasonable fees and costs in bringing this motion in the amount of $ 4,967.50 pursuant to CCP § 396b(b). Plaintiff unreasonably denied Defendants' request for a transfer of venue on September 23, 2013. Plaintiff did not agree to stipulate to a transfer until after Defendant filed this motion and already incurred the fees and costs associated with preparing and filing this motion. Plaintiff should have known San Francisco was not a proper venue. The Complaint pleads that Defendants reside in San Diego. (Complaint, 6-8.) The contract does not specify San Francisco as the location of performance. (CCP § 395(a).) Plaintiff has cited no case that establishes that a physical injury occurs for venue purposes at a website's headquarters when the operation of a website or the website is injured. A venue selection provision in a contract will not be enforced when there is no statutory basis for that venue. (Battaglia Ent., Inc. v. Super. Ct. (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 309, 317.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/ehg
